quality.rationale: 相对论动理论矩积分推导+热力学状态方程证明+完整解答

origin.question:
Problem 5.34. The invariant equilibrium distribution function of a relativistic gas is (25-1) $ rac{1}{4}$ .
$$ \mathcal {N} \left(\mathrm {p}^{\alpha}, \mathrm {x}^{\alpha}ight) \equiv  rac {\mathrm {d N}}{\mathrm {d}^{3} \mathrm {x d}^{3} \mathrm {P}} =  rac {(2 \mathrm {J} + 1) / \mathrm {h}^{3}}{\exp \left[ -  rac {\mathrm {P} \cdot \mathrm {u}}{\mathrm {k T}} - 	hetaight] - \varepsilon}. $$
Here $\mathbf{J} =$ spin of particles, $\mathbf{h} =$ Planck's constant, $\mathbf{u} =$ mean 4-velocity of gas, $\varepsilon = 1,0$ or $-1$ for Bose-Einstein, Maxwell-Boltzmann or Fermi-Dirac statistics respectively. The parameter $	heta$ is independent of $\mathbf{P}$ . The first two moments of $\mathfrak{N}$ are
$$ \mathrm {J}^{\mu} \equiv \int \mathcal {N} \mathrm {P}^{\mu}  rac {\mathrm {d}^{3} \mathrm {P}}{(- \mathrm {P} \cdot \mathrm {u})}, \qquad \mathrm {T}^{\mu 
u} \equiv \int \mathcal {N} \mathrm {P}^{\mu} \mathrm {P}^{
u}  rac {\mathrm {d}^{3} \mathrm {P}}{(- \mathrm {P} \cdot \mathrm {u})}. $$
Since $\mathbf{u}$ is the only free vector, these integrals must have the form
$$ J^{\mu} = n u^{\mu}, \quad T^{\mu 
u} = (ho + p) u^{\mu} u^{
u} + p g^{\mu 
u}. $$
(This is the kinetic-theory definition of $\mathfrak{n},ho ,\mathfrak{p}$ .)
(d) Show that for a Maxwell-Boltzmann gas, $p = nkT$ for all $T$ .
origin.solution:
(d) For $\varepsilon = 0$
$$ \frac {\mathsf {p}}{\mathsf {n}} = \frac {\frac {\mathsf {m}}{3} \int_{0}^{\inf ty} \sin h^{4} \chi \mathsf {e}^{- \beta \cos h \chi} \mathsf {d} \chi}{\int_{0}^{\inf ty} \sin h^{2} \chi \cos h \chi \mathsf {e}^{- \beta \cos h \chi} \mathsf {d} \chi}, $$
but
$$ \begin{array}{l} \int_{0}^{\inf ty} \sin h^{4} \chi \exp (- \beta \cos h \chi) d \chi = - \frac {1}{\beta} \sin h^{3} \chi \exp (- \beta \cos h \chi) |_{0}^{\inf ty} \\ + \frac {3}{\beta} \int_{0}^{\inf ty} \sin h^{3} \chi \cos h \chi \exp (- \beta \cos h \chi) d \chi . \\ \end{array} $$
The first term on the right vanishes, so
$$ \mathbf {p} = \mathbf {n k T}. $$
